title: Intense Umami Algerian Pork Tenderloin Tagine
description: A romantic Algerian dish featuring pork tenderloin in a rich umami sauce, served en-croute in a cazuela.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the pork tenderloin with salt, black pepper, cumin, and smoked paprika.
Heat the olive oil in the cazuela over medium-high heat and sear the pork tenderloin until browned on all sides.
Remove the pork from the cazuela and set it aside.
Add the chopped onion to the cazuela and cook until it is softened and translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the cazuela and cook for 1 minute.
Add the rehydrated porcini mushrooms and their liquid to the cazuela and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the chicken broth to the cazuela and bring to a boil.
Return the pork tenderloin to the cazuela and cover it with a lid or foil.
Transfer the cazuela to the preheated oven and bake for 20 minutes.
Remove the cazuela from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving.
